text: What if I told you plants have a secret highway? This highway is called phloem, and it transports sugars throughout the plant. Imagine a bustling city, where every cell is a destination, and phloem is the delivery truck. Through pressure flow, sugars move from leaves to roots, fueling growth and repair. But here's the twist—sometimes, this system can fail. Understanding phloem transport not only helps us appreciate plants but also their survival strategies. Curious about more plant secrets? Follow for more insights into our green friends!
